tldr: Massive capital project update (~$200M in development), FY27 budget strategy session emphasizing no tax increase, Hurricane Helene recovery update, bookmobile generator funding, AV system upgrade, and cybersecurity pilot program. Owen Carson announced candidacy for commission and pushed for County Planner hire. Board recognized Clerk Trisha Hogan's Master Municipal Clerk designation.

keyVotes.description: Letter to GA for additional virtual school days
GovDeals contract for foreclosed property auction

topics.text: Pushed for ¼-cent sales tax referendum timing discussion
Asked about bookmobile lifespan and original funding; encouraged grant exploration for replacement
Confirmed vacant County Planner position will be filled
Supported organizing capital advocacy requests by category
Shared condolences for retired educator Kathy Haehnel
Asked whether Rosman old gym issues affect retaining wall project
Raised concerns about committing to $36M courthouse estimate before final design/bidding
Noted property tax reform is growing national topic, cited NCACC meeting with Florida reps
Asked about NCDPI review timelines for school capital projects
Asked if AV system could relocate to historic courthouse
Asked whether cybersecurity staffing is sufficient for SecureNC pilot
Promoted Veterans History Museum info and Rising Above Helene documentary screening
Drove letter to GA advocating more virtual school days for weather-affected WNC districts
Supported SMART goals in nonprofit grant funding for outcome tracking
Suggested adding Starlink to bookmobile for emergency connectivity
Cautioned public about social media misinformation re: county vs NCDOT responsibilities
Credited long-term legislative relationships for county's grant success
Recommended prioritizing capital advocacy: courthouse first, then BRCC campus
Thanked staff for improved capital project reporting transparency
Advocated differentiated tax rate on vacation/second homes like VA, TN, SC
Pushed for increased federal PILT funding — 2/3 of county land doesn't generate property tax
Supported parcel fee model for solid waste (like SC) as alternative to tipping fees
Asked about Solid Waste Study timeline
Asked about structural cause of Rosman old gym issues — drainage + corrosion
Supported including max projects in capital advocacy letter
Congratulated 911 Center on full staffing after years of shortages
Credited county advocacy in Raleigh for $7M landfill grant
Supported satellite Fire/EMS facility and Quebec EMS station in advocacy
Asked about putting ¼-cent sales tax back on 2026 ballot
Floated that sales tax + fee-based solid waste could cut property taxes ~3 cents
Asked if solid waste rate study would support full cost recovery through fees
Expressed appreciation for improved capital project reporting detail

publicComments.speaker: Owen Carson
publicComments.summary: Asked about County Planner position status; pushed for hire to implement Housing Plan; requested advisory board minutes be posted online; announced candidacy for Commission

publicComments.speaker: Doug Powell
publicComments.summary: Shared Black History Month remarks on African American contributions during the Revolutionary era
